title: Tutorial - Inspecting a running container multiline bash fails
body:
### Issue Description

Hello, 

I've been folowing the tutorial https://github.com/containers/podman/blob/main/docs/tutorials/podman_tutorial.md

and the command in [Inspecting a running container ](https://github.com/containers/podman/blob/main/docs/tutorials/podman_tutorial.md#inspecting-a-running-container) fails on my bash shell. 
(

### Steps to reproduce the issue

Steps to reproduce the issue
1. execute this shell command 
 
``` shell
podman inspect basic_httpd | grep IPAddress\":
            "SecondaryIPAddresses": null,
            "IPAddress": "",
```
2.
3.


### Describe the results you received

> $ podman inspect basic_httpd | grep IPAddress\":
            "SecondaryIPAddresses": null,
            "IPAddress": "",
               "IPAddress": "",
bash: SecondaryIPAddresses:: command not found
bash: IPAddress:: command not found


### Describe the results you expected

I expected a result form podman instpect, not an error from the shell 

### podman info output

```yaml
$ podman ps
CONTAINER ID  IMAGE                           COMMAND               CREATED         STATUS             PORTS                 NAMES
f4e588be636b  docker.io/library/nginx:latest  nginx -g daemon o...  18 minutes ago  Up 18 minutes ago  0.0.0.0:8080->80/tcp  basic_httpd
```

### Podman in a container

No

### Privileged Or Rootless

None

### Upstream Latest Release

No

### Additional environment details

irrelevent

### Additional information

Debian 12 
tested with Ghostty and Terminal terminal apps

comments.body: The two lines are the output of the command, you should not paste them as command.

Oh ! Thanks, I didn't see that coming, the indentation made me think it was the continuation of the previous line.

May be, not in the same block or commented out ? 

Best
